title: Executive Order 9204—Coordination of Federal Activities Affecting the Fishery Industry

flags.justification: A liaison and coordination role, not direct regulatory command over the fishery industry; functions are limited to information exchange and recommendations.
The Coordinator's functions are specifically defined as liaison and advisory in nature, reasonably bounded.
Coordination and advisory functions with no binding compliance regime; industry cooperation is voluntary.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.

Sec. 4 explicitly ties the order's duration to the underlying statute: it 'shall continue in force and effect so long as Title I of the First War Powers Act, 1941, remains in force' — a clean, appropriately-scoped, statute-linked sunset.
This creates a new coordination and information-sharing structure rather than removing any existing oversight.
A voluntary, advisory coordination structure with no compliance regime requiring third-party monitoring.
A narrow, wartime-specific coordination function tied to a temporary statute.
Each relevant agency designates its own liaison officer rather than the Fishery Coordinator seizing control of their functions — a cooperative, well-designed structure.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/14 = 0.0%. A well-designed wartime coordination order, notable for explicitly linking its own duration to the underlying statute's own force rather than to a self-determined or indefinite emergency declaration.
